seed_problem: ALE-Bench pickup-delivery TSP (ahc006/027/039); Frontier-CS routing: choose/serve precedence pickup-delivery pairs on one depot-to-depot tour, checker validates precedence + returns tour-length ratio.
mutation: composite: skin as a satellite-ground-station downlink tender and turn the selective single-vehicle pickup-delivery TSP into a LARGE-scale MANDATORY-service capacitated precedence tour (all N jobs must be served) under rounded-Euclidean distance; the objective is pure tour length and the calibrated baseline B is the index-order serialized plan, so batching under capacity (not request selection) is the source of hardness and strategy divergence.
title: Downlink Tender: Precedence-Constrained Cartridge Ferry Tour
strategies: serialized baseline: serve jobs one at a time in index order (pickup i, deliver i) -- reproduces the checker baseline B, ratio = 0.1
sequential-nearest-request greedy: repeatedly serve the job whose pickup is nearest to the current position, fully (pickup then deliver) before the next -- shortens travel but never batches

nearest-feasible-event greedy with capacity batching: at each step move to the nearest of {a pickup that still fits the rack, a delivery of an on-board cartridge}, taking the better of this and the sequential tour
cheapest-insertion of pickup/delivery pairs plus feasibility-checked 2-opt / or-opt local search respecting precedence and capacity
